Scenario: Email from 'portal@oauife-students.com': 'IMPORTANT: Your OAU student portal session has expired due to inactivity. Re-activate at oauife-student-portal.com with your matric number and portal password within 24 hours or lose access to your academic records.'
OptionA: Re-activate immediately — losing access to academic records is serious
OptionB: Go directly to your university's official portal URL (check your offer letter or university website)
OptionC: Reply asking for the correct portal address
CorrectAnswer: Go directly to your university's official portal URL (check your offer letter or university website)
Hint: University portals have fixed official URLs on your offer letter and official university website. 'oauife-students.com' and 'oauife-student-portal.com' are not OAU's domain (oauife.edu.ng).
Explanation: Student portal phishing hits during critical academic periods (exam registration, result checking). The academic records threat creates maximum fear for students. Always use bookmarked or offer-letter-specified portal URLs.

Scenario: SMS: 'NECO: Your SSCE certificate is ready for collection. A ₦8,000 logistics fee is required. Pay at neco-certificate.com and upload proof of payment to receive your certificate dispatch date.'
OptionA: Pay the fee — your NECO certificate is important for your future
OptionB: Visit neco.gov.ng or call NECO on 0800-CALL-NECO to verify certificate collection processes
OptionC: Pay ₦8,000 — it's worth it for your certificate
CorrectAnswer: Visit neco.gov.ng or call NECO on 0800-CALL-NECO to verify certificate collection processes
Hint: NECO certificate collection is done through official channels at NECO offices and registered secondary schools. No fees are collected via third-party websites.
Explanation: Certificate collection smishing targets school leavers and their families during results season. ₦8,000 is within budget and the certificate is highly valuable, making the ask feel proportionate.

Scenario: Call: 'Hello, this is the NYSC orientation camp coordinator in your posting state. We have accommodation for you but need to confirm your NIN and phone number for the final list. Also, a ₦12,000 bedspace reservation fee must be paid to this account before 5pm today or your space will be given to the next person.'
OptionA: Pay and confirm immediately — NYSC accommodation is important
OptionB: Hang up and call the official NYSC helpline at 09-292-000-1 to verify accommodation processes
OptionC: Pay ₦5,000 as a deposit to hold the space while you verify
CorrectAnswer: Hang up and call the official NYSC helpline at 09-292-000-1 to verify accommodation processes
Hint: NYSC orientation camp accommodation is assigned, not reserved for fees. All NYSC official communication goes through the NYSC portal and official call-up letter.
Explanation: NYSC accommodation scams exploit corps members' anxiety about orientation camp. NYSC has never charged bedspace reservation fees — accommodation is part of the NYSC programme.
PsychPrinciple: Institutional Anxiety + Urgency + Advance Fee Fraud

Scenario: An email claiming to be from a lecturing colleague: 'Hi, I'm presenting at a conference next week on cybersecurity in Nigerian universities. Can you share access to the university's network monitoring dashboard for 30 minutes so I can capture some anonymised screenshots for my presentation slides?'
OptionA: Grant access temporarily — supporting academic research is positive
OptionB: Decline and offer to provide officially anonymised screenshots yourself through proper data sharing channels
OptionC: Grant view-only access under supervision
CorrectAnswer: Decline and offer to provide officially anonymised screenshots yourself through proper data sharing channels
Hint: Network monitoring dashboard access is privileged. 'Temporary' access still creates a complete snapshot of your network topology, active sessions, and security posture. Offer to produce anonymised visuals yourself.
Explanation: Academic Pretexting targets IT staff using Collegial Trust. Network monitoring data is among the most sensitive IT assets — it reveals network architecture, security tools, and active sessions that enable targeted attacks.

Scenario: Email to hospital staff: 'WEBINAR INVITATION: Free CPD-accredited webinar on Electronic Medical Records Best Practices. Register at: cmr-healthcare-ng.com/register with your hospital staff email and work password to access the live session.'
OptionA: Register for the free CPD points — continuous professional development is required
OptionB: Contact the CPD body directly to verify the webinar through official channels
OptionC: Register but use a simple password for this account only
CorrectAnswer: Contact the CPD body directly to verify the webinar through official channels
Hint: Legitimate CPD webinars use established platforms (Zoom, Microsoft Teams) and never ask for your hospital staff email and work password on a third-party registration site.
Explanation: Healthcare CPD phishing exploits the mandatory professional development requirement. Your hospital email and work password combination is a premium target — it grants access to patient records, clinical systems, and internal communications.

Scenario: Ministry email: 'IT SECURITY NOTICE: A mandatory Windows security patch must be installed on all ministry computers by Friday. Download the patch at: ministry-security-patch.com and run as administrator. Reference: NITDA/SEC/2025/018.'
OptionA: Download and install the patch immediately — security patches are mandatory
OptionB: Contact your ministry's official IT helpdesk to verify the patch and receive it through official channels
OptionC: Only install if your antivirus does not flag it
CorrectAnswer: Contact your ministry's official IT helpdesk to verify the patch and receive it through official channels
Hint: Ministry IT patches are distributed through official software deployment systems (WSUS, SCCM) or IT helpdesk — never from external commercial domains. 'ministry-security-patch.com' is not a government domain.
Explanation: Security patch phishing is particularly effective because IT compliance is a real institutional requirement. Running a malicious executable as administrator gives it complete system control.
PsychPrinciple: False IT Compliance + Administrative Privilege Escalation

Scenario: SMS: 'OAGF PAYROLL: Your October salary has been credited but requires BVN confirmation within 4 hours to avoid reversal back to the payroll pool. Confirm at: oagf-salary-confirm.com.'
OptionA: Confirm immediately before your salary is reversed
OptionB: Log into your IPPIS self-service portal directly to check your salary credit status
OptionC: Call your Accounts department to verify
CorrectAnswer: Log into your IPPIS self-service portal directly to check your salary credit status
Hint: Salary credits on IPPIS are permanent once processed — there is no 'confirmation window' or reversal mechanism based on BVN confirmation via SMS. Your IPPIS portal shows your real payroll status.
Explanation: Salary confirmation smishing exploits the financial anxiety of civil servants. The 'reversal' threat is fabricated — the IPPIS payroll system has no such mechanism. Your IPPIS portal is the only authoritative payroll record.

Scenario: Call to your desk: 'Good morning, I'm from the SERVICOM team conducting a quarterly service delivery audit. I need to walk through your computer to see how quickly it responds to service requests. Can you share your screen with me using this link: screen-share-audit.com?'
OptionA: Share your screen — SERVICOM audits are a government-mandated process
OptionB: Tell the caller you'll escalate to your director first before sharing any screen with an external party
OptionC: Share your screen but close sensitive documents first
CorrectAnswer: Tell the caller you'll escalate to your director first before sharing any screen with an external party
Hint: Screen sharing tools from external domains give the remote party full visibility of your screen including any government documents, passwords, and applications you open. SERVICOM does not conduct remote screen-share audits.
Explanation: Screen-share attacks give real-time access to your entire computer. Closing 'sensitive documents' doesn't prevent the attacker from seeing what you type (passwords) or what else is on your screen. Escalation through your director is the correct procedure for any audit request.

Scenario: You find a printed memo on the ministry notice board: 'Staff are reminded to update their IPPIS records using the new portal. Scan the QR code below or visit: ippis-new-portal.com. Update deadline: Friday.'
OptionA: Scan the QR code and update your IPPIS records before the deadline
OptionB: Report the memo to your IT department and check for any official IPPIS update notices through official channels only
OptionC: Visit the URL from your personal phone instead of your work computer
CorrectAnswer: Report the memo to your IT department and check for any official IPPIS update notices through official channels only
Hint: Physical memo baiting places fraudulent notices in trusted physical spaces. IPPIS update notifications come through official ministry communications — not printed memos with QR codes. 'ippis-new-portal.com' is not an official government domain.
Explanation: Physical social engineering in government offices is documented. A printed notice with official-looking formatting exploits Environmental Trust. Scanning the QR code leads to a credential-harvesting page for IPPIS credentials.

Scenario: A retired senior colleague calls: 'You know me — I worked in this ministry for 20 years. I'm trying to get my pension gratuity processed but there's a technical hold. The PFA says they need a letter from a current staff confirming my years of service. Can you just email me a copy of your staff email header on official letterhead? I just need the signature block.'
OptionA: Email the signature block — helping a former colleague is a good deed
OptionB: Explain that official letters must go through your HR department and cannot be created informally
OptionC: Send a personal email confirming the service without official letterhead
CorrectAnswer: Explain that official letters must go through your HR department and cannot be created informally
Hint: Official correspondence can only be generated through HR. An unofficial letter or signature block can be used to forge official documents.
Explanation: Colleague Pretexting exploits Reciprocity and Long-term Trust. An email signature block with official headers can be doctored to create fraudulent ministry letters for pension fraud or other financial crimes.
PsychPrinciple: Reciprocity + Document Forgery Risk + Long-term Trust

Scenario: Email: 'Luno Nigeria: We have detected unusual withdrawal activity on your Luno account. For your security, your account has been temporarily restricted. Verify your identity at luno-ng-verify.com to restore full access within 24 hours.'
OptionA: Verify immediately to restore access to your crypto
OptionB: Go directly to luno.com in your browser and log into your account to check for any restrictions
OptionC: Reply to the email with your verification details
CorrectAnswer: Go directly to luno.com in your browser and log into your account to check for any restrictions
Hint: Luno's domain is luno.com. 'luno-ng-verify.com' is a fraudulent domain. Crypto exchange phishing is extremely dangerous because transfers are irreversible.
Explanation: Crypto account phishing mimics legitimate exchange security alerts. Credential theft from an exchange account enables immediate and irreversible crypto transfer. Always type the exchange URL directly.

Scenario: A recruiter on LinkedIn offers you a 'Senior Blockchain Developer' role. The 'technical assessment' is a GitHub repo containing a smart contract to review and deploy on testnet. The contract contains a function that requests wallet approval permissions during deployment.
OptionA: Complete the technical assessment — it's a legitimate skills test
OptionB: Review the contract code carefully offline first and never grant wallet approvals to unknown contracts
OptionC: Deploy on testnet only — test networks have no real value
CorrectAnswer: Review the contract code carefully offline first and never grant wallet approvals to unknown contracts
Hint: Smart contract deployments requesting wallet approval permissions can drain your connected wallet. Even testnets can be used to test attack mechanisms before targeting mainnet.
Explanation: Developer job scams target blockchain professionals specifically. Malicious smart contracts requesting wallet approvals drain the wallet used to deploy. Contract review before any deployment or approval is fundamental security hygiene.

Scenario: WhatsApp: 'Join our verified crypto signal group where our AI trading bot has made members an average 40% monthly return for 6 months. Minimum investment ₦20,000. All payments to this USDT address. 50 slots only.'
OptionA: Invest ₦20,000 — 40% monthly from an AI bot sounds credible
OptionB: Decline — 40% monthly (480% annual) is statistically impossible from legitimate trading signals
OptionC: Invest a smaller test amount of ₦5,000
CorrectAnswer: Decline — 40% monthly (480% annual) is statistically impossible from legitimate trading signals
Hint: AI trading bots do not reliably produce guaranteed monthly returns. 40% monthly compounded would mean 100x growth per year — no legitimate investment produces this.
Explanation: Crypto signal group scams use AI credibility (modern Ponzi framing) and Social Proof (claimed 6-month track record). 'Limited slots' creates Scarcity. USDT addresses are untraceable once sent.

Scenario: Call: 'Hi, I'm a customer success manager from Binance Nigeria. Our records show you have accumulated 0.08 BTC in trading rewards that have not been claimed. To release them, I need to verify your account — can you confirm your email address and the 2FA code currently showing on your Authenticator app?'
OptionA: Provide the 2FA code quickly — unclaimed rewards expire
OptionB: Hang up — Binance staff never ask for your 2FA code over the phone
OptionC: Confirm your email only, not the 2FA code
CorrectAnswer: Hang up — Binance staff never ask for your 2FA code over the phone
Hint: 2FA codes are one-time credentials that authorise the specific login attempt currently in progress. A caller asking for your current 2FA code is conducting a real-time relay attack against your Binance account.
Explanation: This is a real-time relay attack — the attacker is simultaneously logging into your Binance account while asking you for the 2FA code. As soon as you read the code, they enter it. Binance never requires 2FA codes in outbound calls.

Scenario: Email: 'CoinGecko API Developer Notice: Your API key has exceeded rate limits and will be suspended unless you upgrade to a paid tier. Log into your developer dashboard at coingecko-dev-portal.com to add billing information and restore API access.'
OptionA: Log in and upgrade immediately — API access is needed for your project
OptionB: Go directly to coingecko.com and log into your developer account from the official site
OptionC: Reply to the email asking for an extension
CorrectAnswer: Go directly to coingecko.com and log into your developer account from the official site
Hint: CoinGecko's domain is coingecko.com. 'coingecko-dev-portal.com' is a fraudulent domain targeting developers. Developer account credentials often provide access to production applications.
Explanation: Developer-targeted crypto API phishing steals credentials that may control production financial applications. Developer accounts with billing access provide both credential theft and potential financial fraud vectors.

Scenario: Your NHIS claims portal shows a pending reimbursement of ₦250,000. To receive payment, verify your account at nhis-reimbursement.com within 48 hours.
OptionA: Claim the reimbursement via the link
OptionB: Check nhis.gov.ng directly for any pending claims
OptionC: Reply asking for payment details
CorrectAnswer: Check nhis.gov.ng directly for any pending claims
Hint: NHIS reimbursements are processed through the official NHIS portal (nhis.gov.ng), not third-party websites.
Explanation: Healthcare reimbursement phishing targets workers expecting legitimate claims. NHIS.gov.ng is the only authorised portal for healthcare claims.

Scenario: Email: 'WOFAN Medical Grant: You have been shortlisted for a healthcare professional development grant of ₦75,000. Complete registration at wofan-medgrant.com to claim your award.'
OptionA: Register immediately to claim the grant
OptionB: Verify the grant through WOFAN's official website or contact their office directly
OptionC: Forward to colleagues who also need grants
CorrectAnswer: Verify the grant through WOFAN's official website or contact their office directly
Hint: Legitimate grants are announced through official organisational channels and verified portals — not unsolicited emails to unknown recipients.
Explanation: Grant phishing exploits Healthcare Professionals' need for professional development funding. Unsolicited grant offers always require independent verification.

Scenario: Call: 'I'm from the Pharmacy Council of Nigeria. Your pharmacist registration shows an outstanding annual renewal fee of ₦18,000. If unpaid before our batch deadline today, your registration is automatically suspended. Pay to account number 0344345777 now.'
OptionA: Pay immediately to avoid suspension
OptionB: Hang up and call PCN directly on the number from pcnng.org
OptionC: Ask for a payment receipt before transferring
CorrectAnswer: Hang up and call PCN directly on the number from pcnng.org
Hint: PCN renewal fees are paid through official PCN portals and licensed banks — not over the phone to personal accounts.
Explanation: Pharmacist registration fraud targets a professional's fear of losing their licence. PCN has a documented renewal process on pcnng.org. Account numbers provided over the phone should never be trusted.

Scenario: Call: 'This is the Joint Medical Staff Union rep. We are collecting members' biodata for the new healthcare salary scale update. I need your NIN, salary grade level, and bank account for the new payment template.'
OptionA: Provide the details to ensure you receive the correct salary
OptionB: Verify with your hospital's HR or union chapter before providing any personal data

Scenario: SMS: 'MTN: Port your number to our new network architecture. To complete the migration, reply with your 6-digit porting PIN and NIN. Migration is free — valid for 24 hours only.'
OptionA: Reply with your porting PIN and NIN
OptionB: Never provide a porting PIN via SMS — call MTN on their customer care line to verify any migration
OptionC: Reply with only your NIN
CorrectAnswer: Never provide a porting PIN via SMS — call MTN on their customer care line to verify any migration
Hint: Porting PINs are generated on your request to authorise SIM porting — providing one to an SMS sender directly authorises porting your number away from you.
Explanation: Porting PIN smishing is a direct SIM swap attack. Your porting PIN authorises moving your number to a new SIM under attacker control, rerouting all OTPs.

Scenario: A verified-looking Twitter/X account with 50,000 followers DMs you: 'We are running a social media ambassador programme. We'll pay ₦30,000/week for posting our content. Send your bank details and a selfie with your ID to claim your slot.'
OptionA: Apply — ₦30,000 weekly for social media posts is a good income
OptionB: Verify the company's legitimacy through official channels before sending any personal data
OptionC: Send bank details but not your ID photo
CorrectAnswer: Verify the company's legitimacy through official channels before sending any personal data
Hint: Follower count and verification badges can be purchased or spoofed. Legitimate ambassador programmes have application portals — not DM-based data collection.
Explanation: Brand ambassador scams collect identity documents for money mule recruitment or identity fraud. Your selfie + ID + bank details is a complete identity package.

Scenario: WhatsApp: 'Congratulations! Your TikTok video has 1 million views milestone. Claim your ₦150,000 TikTok Creator Fund payment by verifying at tiktok-creators-ng.com with your TikTok login.'
OptionA: Claim your payment — you worked hard for those views
OptionB: Open TikTok directly and check your Creator Fund dashboard for any actual payments
OptionC: Verify using a dummy TikTok account
CorrectAnswer: Open TikTok directly and check your Creator Fund dashboard for any actual payments
Hint: TikTok Creator Fund payments are processed within the TikTok app. 'tiktok-creators-ng.com' is not TikTok's domain. Creator Fund payments come without any external login required.
Explanation: Creator monetisation phishing targets content creators aspiring to income from their platform work. Credential theft from TikTok can enable account hijacking, content theft, and impersonation.

Scenario: SMS: 'Your Konga order #KGA-83733 has been returned to our warehouse. To redeliver, confirm your address and pay ₦2,500 redelivery fee at konga-redeliver.com within 24hrs.'
OptionA: Pay the fee to get your order redelivered
OptionB: Check konga.com directly for your actual order status
OptionC: Pay ₦1,000 as partial redelivery fee
CorrectAnswer: Check konga.com directly for your actual order status
Hint: Konga's real domain is konga.com. 'konga-redeliver.com' is fraudulent. Redelivery fees are never collected via SMS links — check your actual order on konga.com.
Explanation: Delivery redelivery smishing is common because online shoppers frequently have packages in transit. ₦2,500 seems proportionate for redelivery, making the ask feel legitimate.

Scenario: Call: 'I'm a buyer who saw your item on Jiji. I want to pay via PalmPay transfer but my bank requires I call you to confirm your full account details including your PalmPay PIN for the inbound transfer to work correctly.'
OptionA: Provide the details — confirming a payment PIN for incoming transfers sounds technical
OptionB: Refuse — PalmPay PINs are never needed by the sender for an incoming transfer
OptionC: Provide your account number but not your PIN
CorrectAnswer: Refuse — PalmPay PINs are never needed by the sender for an incoming transfer
Hint: Incoming bank transfers require only your account number — never your PIN. Any 'technical requirement' for your PIN from a buyer is fraud.
Explanation: Marketplace buyer PIN fraud is common on Jiji and other platforms. Incoming transfers require only an account number. Your PIN authorises outgoing transfers — sharing it enables account draining.

Scenario: On Facebook Marketplace, a buyer says: 'I'll pay via PayPal but PayPal to Nigerian accounts requires a processing fee of ₦15,000 paid first to release the funds. Send the fee and I'll transfer the full payment plus the fee back immediately.'
OptionA: Pay the fee — you'll get it back plus your payment
OptionB: Decline — no legitimate payment method requires the seller to pay a fee to receive funds
OptionC: Pay a smaller fee amount to test it
CorrectAnswer: Decline — no legitimate payment method requires the seller to pay a fee to receive funds
Hint: Advance fee fraud on online marketplaces is the direct descendant of '419' scams. No payment platform requires the recipient to pay to receive funds.
Explanation: Marketplace advance fee fraud exploits sellers' desire to complete sales. The promise of immediate repayment creates False Reciprocity. Once the fee is paid, the buyer disappears.

OptionC: Pay a smaller fee amount to test it
CorrectAnswer: Decline — no legitimate payment method requires the seller to pay a fee to receive funds
Hint: Advance fee fraud on online marketplaces is the direct descendant of '419' scams. No payment platform requires the recipient to pay to receive funds.
Explanation: Marketplace advance fee fraud exploits sellers' desire to complete sales. The promise of immediate repayment creates False Reciprocity. Once the fee is paid, the buyer disappears.
